hero.heading: About me
intro: Helping people regain their independence and confidence is what drew me to physiotherapy. For me, rehabilitation is about more than treating an injury - it is about the person behind it. Every patient has their own story, goals and needs, so I listen first and then build a plan that fits them, whether they are recovering from surgery, returning to sport, or rebuilding confidence in their body.

My work is shaped by my own active life. I grew up in Bielsko-Biała, Poland, surrounded by the Beskid Mountains, where I fell in love with running on demanding trails. Outside the clinic you will usually find me at the gym - weightlifting or doing CrossFit - or out running in the mountains. I'll happily try any activity that comes my way, whether that's swimming, yoga, or cross-country skiing. All of this helps me understand what movement means to the people I treat, and what it takes to get them back to it.
hero.photoAlt: Julia Pilch working in the clinic with an exercise ball
